unmet_need: More effective and personalized music discovery features that go beyond current algorithms
example_quotes: Recommendations arrive with no context. Just tell me why this track fits my taste so I can trust it.
Overwhelmed by the firehose of new releases every Friday. Discovery is just too much effort and too high-effort to keep up with.
I'm a power user who digs deep cuts and obscure artists below a certain popularity. Spotify's recommendations and discovery are far too cautious and safe for me.

unmet_need: More control over music discovery, better playlist curation, and increased user engagement
example_quotes: Discover Weekly has gone stale and repetitive. I never discover anything genuinely new from it anymore.
High intent here — I open Discover Weekly, save a couple, then forget them. Nothing ever converts into a habit.
Please add a comfort-to-curiosity control. Let me push the algorithm to be more out there and adventurous when I want.

unmet_need: ability to filter out AI-generated music from recommendations
segment: music enthusiasts/purists who value human-created content
example_quotes: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. Ai music has ruined my Discover Weekly. Looked forward to it every week  😢
Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. Always create your own playlists, folks. Discover music through other sites instead

Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs. I don’t post to stuff like this. Gang….if I can’t filter AI content soon, I’m out. Have loved the service for several years now, but I can’t even listen to my discover weekly anymore.

unmet_need: Users want a music discovery algorithm that surfaces new and diverse music, rather than just recommending songs they already know and like
example_quotes: Thievery Incorporated. The most stupid and vicious IP thefts of hapless artists. Leave this app
The algorithm keeps recycling my own taste back at me. Total echo chamber — I already have and already follow these artists.

Re: Restore Web API write access for individual developers (Development mode) —   follow/playlist en. Hi @dbesoain   Thanks for posting in the Spotify Community! Spotify made a lot of changes to its API. I understand your struggle. According to Migration Guide , all save and follow actions use PUT /me/library now. unmet_need: Control over music discovery and social features
example_quotes: DJ sucks. Love the recommendation algorithm in "DJ", but hate the ai dj. Would love it if we had the option to turn him off

Public Spotify Jams  Spotify Jam. Public Spotify Jams Spotify Jam is a fantastic feature, but it's currently limited to invited users or people nearby. I would love an option to create public Jams that anyone on Spotify could discover and join. Users could browse Jams by genre, mood, country, or activity (study, workout, road trip, etc.). Hosts could choose whether their Jam is Public or Private and still have moderation controls, such as removing participants or limiting who can change the queue. This would make Spotify much more social while helping users discover new music and connect with people who share similar musical tastes

Public Jam. So I had an idea: instead of Jam being private like it is today, why not allow users to make it public to everyone on Spotify, or even limit it to Premium subscribers?   People could discover new songs from other participants, enjoy a shared listening experience, and even chat about the music. Users joining a public Jam could suggest the next song to play or simply listen and enjoy the vibe.   I think this could encourage more people to use the app because listening to music together is fun and creates a sense of community. It would be like a mix between Spotify and a live radio station powered by the listeners themselves.   What do you think? Would you use a feature like this? 🎧 ❤️   If you like this idea, give it a vote! 👍🏻 🎶
